pattern: Sorting pairs the current minimum with the current maximum from opposite ends, and the count of distinct min-plus-max sums equals the count of distinct averages.
hints: Repeatedly removing the smallest and largest is exactly pairing the two ends of the sorted array.
Since each pair divides by the same two, comparing the sums of the min and max is enough to tell averages apart.
Sort, walk a left and right pointer toward the centre, collect each min+max into a set, and return the set's size.

edge_inputs.description: two pairs giving the same average
all equal, only one distinct average
symmetric values collapse to one average

stress.description: random even-length arrays in the allowed value range
